howItWorks.intro: This 401k calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much should I contribute to my 401k?
faq.answer: At minimum, contribute enough to get the full employer match — that is free money. Ideally, aim for 15-20% of salary including the match. The 2026 employee limit is $23,500 ($31,000 if 50+).

faq.question: What is an employer match?
faq.answer: Many employers match your contributions up to a percentage. A 4% match means if you contribute 4% of salary, your employer adds another 4%. Not taking the full match is leaving free money on the table.

faq.question: What return should I expect?
faq.answer: A diversified portfolio historically returns 7-10% before inflation. Target-date funds are a simple option that automatically adjusts risk as you approach retirement.

faq.question: Should I choose Roth or traditional 401k?
faq.answer: Traditional = tax deduction now, pay taxes in retirement. Roth = no deduction now, tax-free withdrawals in retirement. Roth is better if you expect higher taxes later.

howWeCalculate.intro: This 401k calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This 401k calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When planning major financial decisions like buying a home, investing, or taking on debt
When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
When teaching or learning about personal finance and compound growth
